Package simulation

Class CoverageGrid

java.lang.Object
simulation.CoverageGrid
public final class CoverageGrid extends Object
Coarse coverage tracker that divides the simulation field into sectors and records which sectors have been scanned by the UAV.

Used exclusively in the autonomous UAV pass of the A/B comparison to implement frontier-based exploration: instead of following a fixed four-corner perimeter patrol, the autonomous UAV always moves toward the nearest sector whose centre it has not yet scanned, achieving systematic full-field coverage rather than concentrating on the boundary edges.

Sector model

The field is partitioned into a uniform grid of square sectors of size sectorSize × sectorSize cells. A sector is marked visited when the UAV's scan circle (radius droneRange) contains the sector's centre — a conservative but clean definition that avoids partial-coverage ambiguity.

Frontier selection

nearestUnvisitedCenter(int, int) returns the centre of the closest unvisited sector, measured as Euclidean distance from the UAV's current position. Greedy-nearest selection minimises unnecessary travel and naturally produces an outward-expanding coverage path from the UAV's starting location.

Why this beats fixed-corner patrol

The predefined four-corner patrol concentrates the UAV's scan budget along the field perimeter, leaving the interior largely unexplored. Frontier exploration adaptively targets unscanned regions regardless of where they are, ensuring that the autonomous UAV builds a fuller knowledge base before each GA→ACO planning invocation.

  • Constructor Details

    • CoverageGrid

      public CoverageGrid(int fieldWidth, int fieldHeight, int sectorSize)
      Creates a coverage grid for the given field dimensions and sector size.
      Parameters:
      fieldWidth - field width in cells (≥ 1)
      fieldHeight - field height in cells (≥ 1)
      sectorSize - width and height of each sector in cells (≥ 1); setting this equal to droneRange gives one sector per scan-circle diameter — a good default

  • Method Details

    • markScanned

      public void markScanned(int x, int y, int droneRange)
      Marks every sector whose centre lies within droneRange of position (x, y) as visited.

      Only sectors for which the Euclidean distance from (x, y) to the sector centre is ≤ droneRange are marked, matching the UAV's actual scan footprint.

      Parameters:
      x - UAV x (row) position
      y - UAV y (column) position
      droneRange - UAV scan radius in cells

    • nearestUnvisitedCenter

      public int[] nearestUnvisitedCenter(int fromX, int fromY)
      Returns the (x, y) centre of the nearest unvisited sector from position (fromX, fromY), or null if every sector has already been visited.

      Greedy-nearest selection minimises unnecessary travel and naturally produces a spiral-like outward coverage path from the UAV's current location.

      Parameters:
      fromX - UAV x (row) position
      fromY - UAV y (column) position
      Returns:
      int[]{centerX, centerY} of the nearest unvisited sector, or null if all sectors are covered

    • coverageFraction

      public double coverageFraction()
      Returns the fraction of sectors that have been visited (0.0 = none, 1.0 = all).
      Returns:
      coverage fraction in [0.0, 1.0]

    • isSectorVisited

      public boolean isSectorVisited(double x, double y)
      Returns true if the sector containing grid cell (x, y) has already been marked as visited.

      Used by UAV#replan(CoverageGrid) to exclude waypoint candidates whose sector has been fully scanned — routing to those nodes wastes travel time and causes the planner to revisit already-explored regions (local minima).

      Parameters:
      x - x (row) coordinate of the node's last-known position
      y - y (column) coordinate of the node's last-known position
      Returns:
      true if the containing sector is already visited

    • allCovered

      public boolean allCovered()
      Returns:
      true if every sector has been visited

    • totalSectors

      public int totalSectors()
      Returns:
      total number of sectors in the grid

    • getSectorSize

      public int getSectorSize()
      Returns:
      sector width/height in cells

    • getNumRows

      public int getNumRows()
      Returns:
      number of sector rows

    • getNumCols

      public int getNumCols()
      Returns:
      number of sector columns

    • visitedCopy

      public boolean[][] visitedCopy()
      Returns a deep copy of the visited-sector array for GUI rendering. Callers may freely modify the returned array without affecting this grid.
      Returns:
      deep copy of the visited array
